content: Jawad Azeem is a Computer Science student at Virginia Tech (B.S. CS, graduating 2028) focused on backend engineering, cloud infrastructure, and distributed systems. He builds and deploys reliable, scalable systems on AWS — backend services, cloud infrastructure, and distributed architectures. He cares about writing software that's reliable, observable, and built to scale.

Jawad's work spans from designing Spring Boot services deployed on AWS to building event-driven architectures that handle high-concurrency workloads. He is drawn to hard problems — the kind where you need to think carefully about state, failure modes, and system boundaries. His areas of focus include Cloud Infrastructure, Backend Systems, and Distributed Architecture.

Jawad's tech stack includes Java, Spring Boot, AWS, Docker, JavaScript, TypeScript, C++, Python, Node.js, PostgreSQL, REST APIs, Terraform, CI/CD, Linux, Redis, gRPC, Event-Driven architectures, Git, React, Microservices, SQS, Lambda, DynamoDB, CloudFormation, S3, Kubernetes, Scikit-Learn, Pandas, and NumPy.
Jawad holds two AWS certifications: AWS Solutions Architect Associate and AWS Developer Associate.
Jawad is studying Computer Science at Virginia Tech, pursuing a B.S. degree with an expected graduation in 2028. He is located in Virginia, US.

topic: general dynamics mission systems work experience
content: Jawad is currently a Software Systems Engineer Intern at General Dynamics Mission Systems (May 2026 — Present). He contributes to mission-critical submarine payload control software efforts in a secure defense environment. He owns the modernization of UAV applications — containerizing legacy systems and rewriting old codebases to modern frameworks. He also builds new UUV (Unmanned Underwater Vehicle) applications using Java/Spring framework and TypeScript. His tech stack at GDMS includes Java/Spring, C++, and TypeScript.

Jawad was a Software Engineering Intern at CosmicLens, Inc (May 2025 — Aug 2025). He built and deployed Spring Boot services on AWS with production-grade CI/CD pipelines. He modernized ETL pipelines and centralized logging for improved observability. He integrated a serverless RAG-powered chatbot for automated customer support workflows. His tech stack included Spring Boot, AWS, ETL, and Serverless technologies.

Avisos is a real-time IoT monitoring platform powered by Computer Vision AI, Generative AI, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). It delivers intelligent insights and descriptive information about events as they happen. Built on a real IoT device, the system fuses sensor data with AI-driven analysis for actionable, real-time situational awareness. The tech stack includes Java Spring, C++, TypeScript/JS, Python (with Scikit-Learn, Pandas, NumPy), AWS, and Docker. API available at avisos.jawadazeem.com.

Blueprint is a FinOps platform for cloud cost analysis using Generative AI. It directly ingests billing and usage data from GCP, Azure, and AWS, then applies RAG-driven intelligence to surface cost optimization recommendations and actionable spending insights. The tech stack includes Java Spring Boot, Google GenAI, AWS, GCP, Azure, Docker, and CI/CD. API available at blueprint.jawadazeem.com.

Resonance is a high-concurrency backend engine that models business workflows as strict state machines and executes them through a queue-based processing core — with built-in rules, retries, recovery, and full traceability. The tech stack includes AWS SQS, Java Spring Boot, and AWS.
You can reach Jawad via email at jawad.azeem@outlook.com, on GitHub at github.com/jawadazeem, or on LinkedIn at linkedin.com/in/jawad-azeem-5b40b625b/.
